requirements_scoping.reasoning: The response opens with explicit functional requirements (1:1 DMs, groups, channels, media, reactions, threads, search, push notifications), quantified non-functional requirements (P99 < 250ms, 99.99% availability, ordering preserved), and clear out-of-scope declarations (E2EE, full video/audio calling). It also resolves the prompt's ambiguity by explicitly choosing a hybrid WhatsApp/Slack model and justifying the 100M DAU target. The assumptions are stated and justified (server-side indexing for search, enterprise use case).

capacity_estimation.reasoning: The math is fully derived from stated assumptions: DAU → messages/day → peak msg/s with 2× multiplier → weighted fan-out factor (4.3×) derived from conversation mix → peak write load (398K writes/sec). Storage breaks down by tier with replication factors, yielding 95 TB/day and 35 PB/year. Bandwidth is computed end-to-end. Numbers demonstrably drive design decisions: the 4.3× fan-out directly justifies the hybrid write-time/read-time fan-out strategy; the 398K writes/sec justifies Cassandra over PostgreSQL for messages. However, it does not explicitly flag fragile assumptions or sanity-check results (e.g., 'does 35 PB/year make sense?').

architecture_coherence.reasoning: Every component is justified against specific requirements or capacity numbers. WebSocket Gateway handles 30M concurrent connections; Cassandra handles 398K writes/sec; PostgreSQL handles relational metadata needing ACID; hybrid fan-out (inbox vs timeline) directly addresses the 4.3× fan-out calculation. Data flow is traceable end-to-end from client through WSG, Kafka, Chat Service, to appropriate storage tier. No hallucinated services—each has a clear role. The architecture avoids anti-patterns: explicitly does NOT use global sequence numbers, does NOT write per-message read receipts, does NOT fan-out presence updates to all friends.

deep_dive_depth.reasoning: Multiple deep-dives with concrete mechanisms and tradeoffs: (1) Realtime Communication—session binding with Redis TTL, heartbeat protocol, reconnection with sync_checkpoint, and explicit HTTP/2 + WebSocket tradeoff vs WebTransport. (2) Message Routing—exact algorithm for write-time fan-out (unlogged Cassandra batch, idempotency key) and read-time fan-out (Redis Pub/Sub to subscribed WSG nodes), with exception-write for @mentions. (3) Presence—pull-based + delta sync to avoid thundering herd, with explicit acceptance of 60s stale online status. (4) Storage tiering with TTL-based hot/warm/cold. (5) Sync/Ordering—TimeUUID with tie-breaker, cursor-based read receipts. Several surface non-obvious edge cases: Cassandra hot partition mitigation (rate-limit + model migration), presence thundering herd (batched 5s windows), cross-region split brain (synchronous_commit = remote_apply).

tradeoffs_failure_modes.reasoning: Tradeoffs are quantified or concretely scoped throughout: hybrid fan-out chosen because 'write-time is too expensive for 10k channels; read-time is too slow for DMs'; Cassandra + PostgreSQL vs Spanner because 'polyglot persistence' serves different throughput/ACID needs; server-side indexing vs E2EE explicitly sacrifices privacy for enterprise searchability; direct-to-S3 upload saves ~1.4 GB/s and ~70% cost. Failure modes table covers 8 specific scenarios with concrete mitigations, including non-obvious ones: Cassandra hot partition rate-limiting, presence thundering herd with batched windows, push notification flood with Redis Stream buffering and exponential backoff. The design explicitly acknowledges its own limits: 'this design cannot handle E2EE' (out of scope), accepts 60s ghost online status, accepts stale presence during Redis shard failure rather than building consensus.
